L. 101–502“(A) .—Subject to the extent of amounts made available in appropriations Acts, a grant made for purposes of this paragraph for an eligible area shall be made in an amount equal to the sum of—In general“(i) an amount determined in accordance with subparagraph (B); and“(ii) an amount determined in accordance with subparagraph (C).“(B) .—The amount referred to in clause (i) of subparagraph (A) is an amount equal to the product of—Amount relating to cumulative number of cases“(i) an amount equal to 75 percent of the amounts available for distribution under paragraph (2) for the fiscal year involved; and“(ii) a percentage equal to the quotient of—“(I) the cumulative number of cases of acquired immune deficiency syndrome in the eligible area involved, as indicated by the number of such cases reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control on the applicable date described in ; divided bysection 300ff–11(a) of this title“(II) the sum of the cumulative number of such cases in all eligible areas for which an application for a grant under paragraph (1) has been approved.“(C) .—The amount referred to in clause (ii) of subparagraph (A) is an amount equal to the product of—Amount relating to per capita incidence of cases“(i) an amount equal to 25 percent of the amounts available for distribution under paragraph (2) for the fiscal year involved; and“(ii) a percentage developed by the Secretary through consideration of the ratio of—“(I) the per capita incidence of cumulative cases of acquired immune deficiency syndrome in the eligible area involved (computed on the basis of the most recently available data on the population of the area); to“(II) the per capita incidence of such cumulative cases in all eligible areas for which an application for a grant under paragraph (1) has been approved (computed on the basis of the most recently available data on the population of such areas).”

L. 101–502“(A) .—Subject to the extent of amounts made available in appropriations Acts, a grant made for purposes of this paragraph for an eligible area shall be made in an amount equal to the sum of—In general“(i) an amount determined in accordance with subparagraph (B); and“(ii) an amount determined in accordance with subparagraph (C).“(B) .—The amount referred to in clause (i) of subparagraph (A) is an amount equal to the product of—Amount relating to cumulative number of cases“(i) an amount equal to 75 percent of the amounts available for distribution under paragraph (2) for the fiscal year involved; and“(ii) a percentage equal to the quotient of—“(I) the cumulative number of cases of acquired immune deficiency syndrome in the eligible area involved, as indicated by the number of such cases reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control on the applicable date described in ; divided bysection 300ff–11(a) of this title“(II) the sum of the cumulative number of such cases in all eligible areas for which an application for a grant under paragraph (1) has been approved.“(C) .—The amount referred to in clause (ii) of subparagraph (A) is an amount equal to the product of—Amount relating to per capita incidence of cases“(i) an amount equal to 25 percent of the amounts available for distribution under paragraph (2) for the fiscal year involved; and“(ii) a percentage developed by the Secretary through consideration of the ratio of—“(I) the per capita incidence of cumulative cases of acquired immune deficiency syndrome in the eligible area involved (computed on the basis of the most recently available data on the population of the area); to“(II) the per capita incidence of such cumulative cases in all eligible areas for which an application for a grant under paragraph (1) has been approved (computed on the basis of the most recently available data on the population of such areas).”

Effective Date of 2009 Amendment; Revival of SectionFor provisions that repeal by of be effective , that the provisions of this section as in effect on , be revived, and that amendment by sections 3(a), 5(a), 6(b), 7(a), and 8(a)(1), (b)(1)(A), (2)(A), (C), (c)(1) of be applicable to this section as so revived and effective as if enacted on , see section 2(a)(2), (3) of , set out as a note under .section 2(a)(1) of Pub. L. 111–87section 703 of Pub. L. 109–415Pub. L. 111–87Pub. L. 111–87section 300ff–11 of this titleSept. 30, 2009Sept. 30, 2009Sept. 30, 2009

Effective Date of 1996 AmendmentAmendment by sections 3(b)(2), (3), 4, 6(c)(1)(B), and 12(c)(2) of effective , and amendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 104–146section 6(c)(1)(A) of Pub. L. 104–146section 13 of Pub. L. 104–146section 300ff–11 of this titleOct. 1, 1996May 20, 1996

children.heading: Grants based on relative need of area
children.label: a Grants based on relative need of area

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out , the Secretary shall make a grant for each eligible area for which an application under has been approved. Each such grant shall be made in an amount determined in accordance with paragraph (3).section 300ff–11(a) of this titlesection 300ff–15(a) of this title

children.heading: Expedited distribution
children.label: 2 Expedited distribution
children.text: Not later than 60 days after an appropriation becomes available to carry out this subpart for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall, except in the case of waivers granted under section 300ff–15(c) of this title, disburse 66⅔ percent of the amount made available under for carrying out this subpart for such fiscal year through grants to eligible areas under , in accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4).1section 300ff–20(b) of this titlesection 300ff–11(a) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: an amount equal to the amount available for distribution under paragraph (2) for the fiscal year involved; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage constituted by the ratio of the distribution factor for the eligible area to the sum of the respective distribution factors for all eligible areas;

children.heading: Distribution factor
children.label: B Distribution factor
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), the term “distribution factor” means an amount equal to the living cases of HIV/AIDS (reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) in the eligible area involved, as determined under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Requirement of names-based reporting
children.label: i Requirement of names-based reporting
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the number determined under this subparagraph for an eligible area for a fiscal year for purposes of subparagraph (B) is the number of living names-based cases of HIV/AIDS that, as of December 31 of the most recent calendar year for which such data is available, have been reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.heading: Transition period; exemption regarding non-AIDS cases
children.label: ii Transition period; exemption regarding non-AIDS cases
I
children.text: a system was in operation as of , that provides sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases throughout the State in which the area is located, subject to clause (viii); orDecember 31, 2005

children.label: II
children.text: no later than the beginning of fiscal year 2008 or a subsequent fiscal year through fiscal year 2012, the Secretary, in consultation with the chief executive of the State in which the area is located, determines that a system has become operational in the State that provides sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases throughout the State.

children.heading: Requirements for exemption for fiscal year 2007
children.label: iii Requirements for exemption for fiscal year 2007
aa
children.text: the State in which the area is located had submitted to the Secretary a plan for making the transition to sufficiently accurate and reliable names-based reporting of living non-AIDS cases of HIV; or

children.label: bb
children.text: all statutory changes necessary to provide for sufficiently accurate and reliable reporting of such cases had been made; and

children.label: II
children.text: the State had agreed that, by , the State will begin accurate and reliable names-based reporting of such cases, except that such agreement is not required to provide that, as of such date, the system for such reporting be fully sufficient with respect to accuracy and reliability throughout the area.April 1, 2008

children.heading: Requirement for exemption as of fiscal year 2008
children.label: iv Requirement for exemption as of fiscal year 2008
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2008 through 2012, an exemption under clause (ii) for an eligible area applies only if, as of , the State in which the area is located is substantially in compliance with the agreement under clause (iii)(II).April 1, 2008

children.heading: Progress toward names-based reporting
children.label: v Progress toward names-based reporting
children.text: For fiscal year 2009 or a subsequent fiscal year, the Secretary may terminate an exemption under clause (ii) for an eligible area if the State in which the area is located submitted a plan under clause (iii)(I)(aa) and the Secretary determines that the State is not substantially following the plan.

children.heading: Counting of cases in areas with exemptions
children.label: vi Counting of cases in areas with exemptions

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: With respect to an eligible area that is under a reporting system for living non-AIDS cases of HIV that is not names-based (referred to in this subparagraph as “code-based reporting”), the Secretary shall, for purposes of this subparagraph, modify the number of such cases reported for the eligible area in order to adjust for duplicative reporting in and among systems that use code-based reporting.

children.heading: Adjustment rate
children.label: II Adjustment rate
children.text: The adjustment rate under subclause (I) for an eligible area shall be a reduction of 5 percent for fiscal years before fiscal year 2012 (and 6 percent for fiscal year 2012) in the number of living non-AIDS cases of HIV reported for the area.

children.heading: Increased adjustment for certain areas previously using code-based reporting
children.label: III Increased adjustment for certain areas previously using code-based reporting
aa
children.text: for fiscal year 2007, such area was a transitional area;

children.label: bb
children.text: fiscal year 2007 was the first year in which the count of living non-AIDS cases of HIV in such area, for purposes of this section, was based on a names-based reporting system; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the amount of funding that such area received under this part for fiscal year 2007 was less than 70 percent of the amount of funding (exclusive of funds that were identified as being for purposes of the Minority AIDS Initiative) that such area received under such part for fiscal year 2006.

children.label: I
children.text: Names-based reporting under clause (i) applies in a jurisdictional portion of the area, or an exemption under clause (ii) applies in such portion (subject to applicable provisions of this subparagraph), according to whether names-based reporting or code-based reporting is used in such portion.

children.label: II
children.text: If under subclause (I) both names-based reporting and code-based reporting apply in the area, the number of code-based cases shall be reduced under clause (vi).

children.heading: List of eligible areas meeting standard regardingDecember 31, 2005
children.label: viii List of eligible areas meeting standard regardingDecember 31, 2005

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If an eligible area or portion thereof is in a State specified in subclause (II), the eligible area or portion shall be considered to meet the standard described in clause (ii)(I). No other eligible area or portion thereof may be considered to meet such standard.

children.heading: Relevant States
children.label: II Relevant States
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the States specified in this subclause are the following: Alaska, Alabama, Arkansas, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Indiana, Iowa, Idaho, Kansas, Louisiana, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, North Dakota, Nebraska, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Nevada, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Wisconsin, West Virginia, Wyoming, Guam, and the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Rules of construction regarding acceptance of reports
children.label: ix Rules of construction regarding acceptance of reports

children.heading: Cases of AIDS
children.label: I Cases of AIDS
children.text: With respect to an eligible area that is subject to the requirement under clause (i) and is not in compliance with the requirement for names-based reporting of living non-AIDS cases of HIV, the Secretary shall, notwithstanding such noncompliance, accept reports of living cases of AIDS that are in accordance with such clause.

children.heading: Applicability of exemption requirements
children.label: II Applicability of exemption requirements
children.text: The provisions of clauses (ii) through (viii) may not be construed as having any legal effect for fiscal year 2013 or any subsequent fiscal year, and accordingly, the status of a State for purposes of such clauses may not be considered after fiscal year 2012.

children.heading: Program for detecting inaccurate or fraudulent counting
children.label: x Program for detecting inaccurate or fraudulent counting
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program to monitor the reporting of names-based cases for purposes of this subparagraph and to detect instances of inaccurate reporting, including fraudulent reporting.

children.heading: Future fiscal years
children.label: xi Future fiscal years
children.text: For fiscal years beginning with fiscal year 2013, determinations under this paragraph shall be based only on living names-based cases of HIV/AIDS with respect to the area involved.

children.heading: Code-based areas; limitation on increase in grant
children.label: D Code-based areas; limitation on increase in grant
I
children.text: for fiscal year 2007, exceed by more than 5 percent the amount of the grant for the area that would have been made pursuant to this paragraph and paragraph (4) for fiscal year 2006 (as such paragraphs were in effect for such fiscal year) if paragraph (2) (as so in effect) had been applied by substituting “66⅔ percent” for “50 percent”; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each of the fiscal years 2008 through 2012, exceed by more than 5 percent the amount of the grant pursuant to this paragraph and paragraph (4) for the area for the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Use of amounts involved
children.label: ii Use of amounts involved
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2012, amounts available as a result of the limitation under clause (i) shall be made available by the Secretary as additional amounts for grants pursuant to subsection (b) for the fiscal year involved, subject to paragraph (4) and .section 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: For fiscal year 2010, an amount equal to 95 percent of the sum of the amount of the grant made pursuant to paragraph (3) and this paragraph for fiscal year 2009.

children.label: ii
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2011 and 2012, an amount equal to 100 percent of the amount of the grant made pursuant to paragraph (3) and this paragraph for fiscal year 2010.

children.label: iii
children.text: For fiscal year 2013, an amount equal to 92.5 percent of the amount of the grant made pursuant to paragraph (3) and this paragraph for fiscal year 2012.

children.heading: Source of funds for increase
children.label: B Source of funds for increase

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: From the amounts available for carrying out the single program referred to in for a fiscal year (relating to supplemental grants), the Secretary shall make available such amounts as may be necessary to comply with subparagraph (A), subject to .section 300ff–19(d)(2)(C) of this titlesection 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Pro rata reduction
children.label: ii Pro rata reduction
children.text: If the amounts referred to in clause (i) for a fiscal year are insufficient to fully comply with subparagraph (A) for the year, the Secretary, in order to provide the additional funds necessary for such compliance, shall reduce on a pro rata basis the amount of each grant pursuant to this subsection for the fiscal year, other than grants for eligible areas for which increases under subparagraph (A) apply. A reduction under the preceding sentence may not be made in an amount that would result in the eligible area involved becoming eligible for such an increase.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: This paragraph may not be construed as having any applicability after fiscal year 2013.

children.label: A
children.text: contains a report concerning the dissemination of emergency relief funds under subsection (a) and the plan for utilization of such funds;

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrates the need in such area, on an objective and quantified basis, for supplemental financial assistance to combat the HIV epidemic;

children.label: C
children.text: demonstrates the existing commitment of local resources of the area, both financial and in-kind, to combating the HIV epidemic;

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrates the ability of the area to utilize such supplemental financial resources in a manner that is immediately responsive and cost effective;

children.label: E
children.text: demonstrates that resources will be allocated in accordance with the local demographic incidence of AIDS including appropriate allocations for services for infants, children, youth, women, and families with HIV/AIDS;

children.label: F
children.text: demonstrates the inclusiveness of affected communities and individuals with HIV/AIDS;

children.label: G
children.text: demonstrates the manner in which the proposed services are consistent with the local needs assessment and the statewide coordinated statement of need;

children.label: H
children.text: demonstrates the ability of the applicant to expend funds efficiently by not having had, for the most recent grant year under subsection (a) for which data is available, more than 5 percent of grant funds under such subsection canceled, offset under subsection (c)(4), or covered by any waivers under subsection (c)(3); and

children.label: I
children.text: demonstrates success in identifying individuals with HIV/AIDS as described in clauses (i) through (iii) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: the number of individuals who have been tested for HIV/AIDS;

children.label: ii
children.text: of those individuals described in clause (i), the number of individuals who tested for HIV/AIDS who are made aware of their status, including the number who test positive; and

children.label: iii
children.text: of those individuals described in clause (ii), the number who have been referred to appropriate treatment and care.

children.label: i
children.text: The unmet need for such services, as determined under or other community input process as defined under .section 300ff–12(b)(4) of this titlesection 300ff–19(d)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: An increasing need for HIV/AIDS-related services, including relative rates of increase in the number of cases of HIV/AIDS.

children.label: iii
children.text: The relative rates of increase in the number of cases of HIV/AIDS within new or emerging subpopulations.

children.label: iv
children.text: The current prevalence of HIV/AIDS.

children.label: v
children.text: Relevant factors related to the cost and complexity of delivering health care to individuals with HIV/AIDS in the eligible area.

children.label: vi
children.text: The impact of co-morbid factors, including co-occurring conditions, determined relevant by the Secretary.

children.label: vii
children.text: The prevalence of homelessness.

children.label: viii
children.text: The prevalence of individuals described under .section 300ff–12(b)(2)(M) of this title

children.label: ix
children.text: The relevant factors that limit access to health care, including geographic variation, adequacy of health insurance coverage, and language barriers.

children.label: x
children.text: The impact of a decline in the amount received pursuant to subsection (a) on services available to all individuals with HIV/AIDS identified and eligible under this subchapter.

children.heading: Priority in making grants
children.label: C Priority in making grants
children.text: The Secretary shall provide funds under this subsection to an eligible area to address the decline or disruption of all EMA-provided services related to the decline in the amounts received pursuant to subsection (a) consistent with the grant award for the eligible area for fiscal year 2006, to the extent that the factor under subparagraph (B)(x) (relating to a decline in funding) applies to the eligible area.

children.heading: Increased adjustment for certain areas previously using code-based reporting
children.label: D Increased adjustment for certain areas previously using code-based reporting
children.text: For purposes of this subsection for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2012, the Secretary shall deem the applicable number of living cases of HIV/AIDS in an area that were reported to and confirmed by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to be 3 percent higher than the actual number if the conditions described in items (aa) through (cc) of subsection (a)(3)(C)(vi)(III) are all satisfied.

children.label: A
children.text: the failure of any eligible area to submit an application under section 300ff–15(c) of this title; or1

children.label: B
children.text: any eligible area informing the Secretary that such eligible area does not intend to expend the full amount of its grant under such section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The failure of an eligible area to submit an application for an expedited grant under subsection (a)(2) shall not result in such area being ineligible for a grant under this subsection.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: The application of an eligible area submitted under shall contain the assurances required under subsection (a) of such section if such eligible area fails to submit an application for an expedited grant under subsection (a)(2).section 300ff–15(b) of this title

children.heading: Timeframe for obligation and expenditure of grant funds
children.label: c Timeframe for obligation and expenditure of grant funds

children.heading: Obligation by end of grant year
children.label: 1 Obligation by end of grant year
children.text: Effective for fiscal year 2007 and subsequent fiscal years, funds from a grant award made pursuant to subsection (a) or (b) for a fiscal year are available for obligation by the eligible area involved through the end of the one-year period beginning on the date in such fiscal year on which funds from the award first become available to the area (referred to in this subsection as the “grant year for the award”), except as provided in paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Supplemental grants; cancellation of unobligated balance of grant award
children.label: 2 Supplemental grants; cancellation of unobligated balance of grant award
A
children.text: the Secretary shall cancel that unobligated balance of the award, and shall require the eligible area to return any amounts from such balance that have been disbursed to the area; and

children.label: B
children.text: the funds involved shall be made available by the Secretary as additional amounts for grants pursuant to subsection (b) for the first fiscal year beginning after the fiscal year in which the Secretary obtains the information necessary for determining that the balance is required under subparagraph (A) to be canceled, except that the availability of the funds for such grants is subject to subsection (a)(4) and as applied for such year.section 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Formula grants; cancellation of unobligated balance of grant award; waiver permitting carryover
children.label: 3 Formula grants; cancellation of unobligated balance of grant award; waiver permitting carryover
i
children.text: before the end of the grant year, the chief elected official of the area submits to the Secretary a written application for a waiver of the cancellation, which application includes a description of the purposes for which the area intends to expend the funds involved; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary approves the waiver.

children.heading: Expenditure by end of carryover year
children.label: B Expenditure by end of carryover year
i
children.text: The unobligated funds are available for expenditure by the eligible area involved for the one-year period beginning upon the expiration of the grant year (referred to in this subsection as the “carryover year”).

children.label: ii
children.text: If the funds are not expended by the end of the carryover year, the Secretary shall cancel that unexpended balance of the award, and shall require the eligible area to return any amounts from such balance that have been disbursed to the area.

children.heading: Use of cancelled balances
children.label: C Use of cancelled balances
children.text: In the case of any balance of a grant award that is cancelled under subparagraph (A) or (B)(ii), the grant funds involved shall be made available by the Secretary as additional amounts for grants pursuant to subsection (b) for the first fiscal year beginning after the fiscal year in which the Secretary obtains the information necessary for determining that the balance is required under such subparagraph to be canceled, except that the availability of the funds for such grants is subject to subsection (a)(4) and as applied for such year.section 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Corresponding reduction in future grant
children.label: D Corresponding reduction in future grant
I
children.text: the Secretary shall reduce, by the same amount as such unobligated balance (less any amount of such balance that is the subject of a waiver of cancellation under subparagraph (A)), the amount of the grant under such subsection for the first fiscal year beginning after the fiscal year in which the Secretary obtains the information necessary for determining that such balance was unobligated as of the end of the grant year (which requirement for a reduction applies without regard to whether a waiver under subparagraph (A) has been approved with respect to such balance); and

children.label: II
children.text: the grant funds involved in such reduction shall be made available by the Secretary as additional funds for grants pursuant to subsection (b) for such first fiscal year, subject to subsection (a)(4) and ;section 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Relation to increases in grant
children.label: ii Relation to increases in grant
children.text: A reduction under clause (i) for an eligible area for a fiscal year may not be taken into account in applying subsection (a)(4) with respect to the area for the subsequent fiscal year.

children.heading: Authority regarding administration of provisions
children.label: 4 Authority regarding administration of provisions
children.text: In administering paragraphs (2) and (3) with respect to the unobligated balance of an eligible area, the Secretary may elect to reduce the amount of future grants to the area under subsection (a) or (b), as applicable, by the amount of any such unobligated balance in lieu of cancelling such amount as provided for in paragraph (2) or (3)(A). In such case, the Secretary may permit the area to use such unobligated balance for purposes of any such future grant. An amount equal to such reduction shall be available for use as additional amounts for grants pursuant to subsection (b), subject to subsection (a)(4) and . Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to affect the authority of the Secretary under paragraphs (2) and (3), including the authority to grant waivers under paragraph (3)(A). The reduction in future grants authorized under this paragraph shall be notwithstanding the penalty required under paragraph (3)(D) with respect to unobligated funds.section 300ff–20(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Compliance with priorities of HIV planning council
children.label: d Compliance with priorities of HIV planning council
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subpart, the Secretary, in carrying out , may not make any grant under subsection (a) or (b) to an eligible area unless the application submitted by such area under for the grant involved demonstrates that the grants made under subsections (a) and (b) to the area for the preceding fiscal year (if any) were expended in accordance with the priorities applicable to such year that were established, pursuant to , by the planning council serving the area.section 300ff–11(a) of this titlesection 300ff–15 of this titlesection 300ff–12(b)(4)(C) of this title

children.heading: Report on the awarding of supplemental funds
children.label: e Report on the awarding of supplemental funds

children.text: the total amount of supplemental funds available under this section for the year involved;
the amount of supplemental funds used in accordance with the hold harmless provisions of subsection (a)(4);
the amount of supplemental funds disbursed pursuant to subsection (b)(2)(C);
the disbursement of the remainder of the supplemental funds after taking into account the uses described in paragraphs (2) and (3); and
the rationale used for the amount of funds disbursed as described under paragraphs (2), (3), and (4).
